title: grevillea humilis subsp. lucens
intro: Grevillea humilis subsp. lucens, also known as the dwarf silk flower, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used by the Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can help to soothe sore muscles and skin irritations. The health benefits of Grevillea humilis subsp. lucens include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from pain and skin conditions.
